req.title: Manager deletes document
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to delete a document through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-031 requires: (1) a manager can delete a document via UI with mandatory field validation and audit record creation returning 200/201; (2) unauthenticated requests return 401; (3) forbidden roles return 403; (4) validation failures return 400. I derive: a positive EP test covering the happy path (manager deletes existing document, audit written, 200/201); a NEG test for an unauthenticated user (401) and a DT test for role-forbidden (403); a BVA boundary test targeting deletion of a document whose ID sits at the minimum valid boundary (1 character / minimum integer) to probe edge-case identifier handling; and a STC test verifying state transition from 'existing' to 'deleted' (idempotency: second delete attempt on already-deleted document returns 404, confirming the system left the correct terminal state). This yields five tests covering all required heuristics and exceeds the minimum of three.

tests.name: test_manager_deletes_document_happy_path
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A document with a known ID exists in the system
actions: Login as manager via <fixture:manager_session>
Navigate to /documents
Locate the target document row by document_id
Click the delete action for that document
Confirm deletion in the confirmation dialog
Capture the HTTP response from the delete submission
expected: Response body contains the deleted document entity id
Document no longer appears in the /documents list
An audit record exists for the delete action via <helper_fn:get_audit_record>
trace: R-WEB-031: positive outcome - document deleted, audit written, 200/201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_deletes_document_happy_path(
    manager_page,          # <fixture> authenticated Playwright Page as manager
    existing_document,     # <fixture> creates a document, yields {"id": ..., "title": ...}
    get_audit_record,      # <helper_fn> queries audit log by entity_id and action
    api_get_documents,     # <helper_fn> GET /documents, returns list
):
    doc_id = existing_document["id"]
    manager_page.goto("/documents")
    row = manager_page.locator(f"[data-testid='doc-row-{doc_id}']")
    expect(row).to_be_visible()

    with manager_page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/documents" in r.url and r.request.method in ("DELETE", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        row.locator("[data-testid='btn-delete']").click()
        manager_page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(doc_id)

    remaining = api_get_documents()
    assert not any(str(d["id"]) == str(doc_id) for d in remaining)

    audit = get_audit_record(entity_id=doc_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record not found"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_document
preconditions: No active session / user is not authenticated
A document with a known ID exists in the system
actions: Make a DELETE request to /documents/<doc_id> with no auth token
Capture the HTTP response status
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_document(
    base_url,              # <fixture> str base URL of hr-app
    existing_document,     # <fixture> creates document, yields {"id": ...}
    get_audit_record,      # <helper_fn> queries audit log
    api_get_documents,     # <helper_fn> GET /documents with manager creds
):
    doc_id = existing_document["id"]

    resp = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/documents/{doc_id}",
        headers={},   # no Authorization header
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"

    remaining = api_get_documents()
    assert any(str(d["id"]) == str(doc_id) for d in remaining), \
        "Document must still exist after unauthenticated delete attempt"

    audit = get_audit_record(entity_id=doc_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should be written on 401"

tests.name: test_non_manager_role_forbidden_to_delete_document
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'employee' (not manager)
A document with a known ID exists in the system
actions: Authenticate as an employee-role user via <fixture:employee_session>
Submit a DELETE request to /documents/<doc_id> with the employee auth token
Capture the HTTP response status
trace: R-WEB-031: error pathway - 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_non_manager_role_forbidden_to_delete_document(
    base_url,              # <fixture> str
    existing_document,     # <fixture> creates document, yields {"id": ...}
    employee_auth_headers, # <fixture> dict with Authorization header for employee role
    get_audit_record,      # <helper_fn>
    api_get_documents,     # <helper_fn>
):
    doc_id = existing_document["id"]

    resp = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/documents/{doc_id}",
        headers=employee_auth_headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"

    remaining = api_get_documents()
    assert any(str(d["id"]) == str(doc_id) for d in remaining), \
        "Document must still exist after forbidden delete attempt"

    audit = get_audit_record(entity_id=doc_id, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should be written on 403"

tests.name: test_manager_deletes_document_with_minimum_boundary_id
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A document exists whose ID equals the minimum valid identifier value (e.g. 1)
actions: Create or ensure a document with minimum boundary ID via <fixture:min_id_document>
Navigate to /documents
Delete the document with the minimum boundary ID through the UI form
Capture the HTTP response
expected: Response body contains entity id equal to the minimum boundary value
Document with minimum boundary ID is absent from the /documents list
Audit record is written for the minimum boundary ID document
trace: R-WEB-031: boundary value on entity identifier - minimum valid document id
R-WEB-031: positive outcome with boundary input
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MIN_DOC_ID = 1   # boundary: smallest valid document identifier

def test_manager_deletes_document_with_minimum_boundary_id(
    manager_page,          # <fixture>
    min_id_document,       # <fixture> ensures doc with id=MIN_DOC_ID exists, yields {"id": 1}
    get_audit_record,      # <helper_fn>
    api_get_documents,     # <helper_fn>
):
    doc_id = min_id_document["id"]
    assert doc_id == MIN_DOC_ID, "Fixture must supply minimum boundary document"

    manager_page.goto("/documents")
    row = manager_page.locator(f"[data-testid='doc-row-{doc_id}']")
    expect(row).to_be_visible()

    with manager_page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/documents" in r.url and r.request.method in ("DELETE", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        row.locator("[data-testid='btn-delete']").click()
        manager_page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete']").click()

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(MIN_DOC_ID)

    remaining = api_get_documents()
    assert not any(str(d["id"]) == str(MIN_DOC_ID) for d in remaining)

    audit = get_audit_record(entity_id=MIN_DOC_ID, action="DELETE")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record missing for boundary-id delete"

tests.name: test_already_deleted_document_returns_terminal_state
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
A document has already been deleted (terminal state: DELETED)
actions: Obtain manager auth headers via <fixture:manager_auth_headers>
Perform a first DELETE request on an existing document (transition: EXISTING -> DELETED)
Assert first response is 200 or 201
Perform a second DELETE request on the same already-deleted document id
Capture the second HTTP response status
expected: First DELETE returns 200 or 201 (transition to DELETED state)
Second DELETE returns 404 (document not found in terminal state)
Exactly one audit record exists for the document id with action DELETE
trace: R-WEB-031: state transition - EXISTING -> DELETED; re-delete on DELETED -> 404
R-WEB-031: idempotency verification; audit record written exactly once
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_already_deleted_document_returns_terminal_state(
    base_url,              # <fixture>
    existing_document,     # <fixture>
    manager_auth_headers,  # <fixture> dict with Authorization header for manager
    get_audit_records,     # <helper_fn> returns list of matching audit records
):
    doc_id = existing_document["id"]

    resp1 = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/documents/{doc_id}",
        headers=manager_auth_headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp1.status_code in (200, 201), \
        f"First delete expected 200/201, got {resp1.status_code}"

    resp2 = requests.delete(
        f"{base_url}/documents/{doc_id}",
        headers=manager_auth_headers,
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert resp2.status_code == 404, \
        f"Second delete on deleted doc expected 404, got {resp2.status_code}"

    audits = get_audit_records(entity_id=doc_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audits) == 1, \
        f"Expected exactly 1 audit record, found {len(audits)}"
